title: Guidance on veterinary antimicrobial sales reporting - Canada.ca
content:
## Guidance on veterinary antimicrobial sales reporting
Drug Establishment Licensing Bulletin #147, March 13, 2023
The Veterinary Antimicrobial Sales Reporting (VASR) system, which collects sales of veterinary antimicrobials important in human medicine from importers, manufacturers and compounders, has opened. The 2022 reporting cycle marks the fifth year of data collection since the regulations came into force.
Individuals must report annual sales for drugs:
- intended for animal use, containing active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) set out on List A
The deadline to report sales for 2022 is March 31, 2023 .
(may include veterinarians or pharmacists) are anyone who compounds a final product for veterinary use with any of the following inputs:
- an API set out on List A, such as the raw ingredient
- a human drug in dosage form containing an API set out on List A for example, products with a drug identification number
- for example, products with a drug identification number
- a veterinary drug in dosage form containing an API set out on List A, especially when compounded for use in food-producing animals for example, products with a drug identification number
- for example, products with a drug identification number
Contact us if you need:
- instructions on how to use the system
- to be added to the system to report data
- to change your contact information in the system
Email: vasr-vavr@hc-sc.gc.ca
- Veterinary antimicrobial sales reporting
- List A: List of certain antimicrobial active pharmaceutical ingredients
- Regulations Amending the Food and Drug Regulations (Veterinary Drugs — Antimicrobial Resistance)
